prompt: New construction commissioning differs from retrocommissioning because new Cx:

options.text: Integrates verification throughout design and construction before turnover
Occurs only after all systems are demolished
Never includes design phase reviews
Eliminates functional testing requirements

explanation: New Cx starts early with OPR, submittals, and TAB/FPT before occupancy.

distractorExplanations.b: New Cx precedes operation of new systems.
distractorExplanations.c: Design review is a core new Cx activity.

prompt: IPMVP Core Concepts require M&V plans to address:

options.text: Baseline, reporting period, adjustments, measurement uncertainty, and savings computation
Only contractor marketing slogans
Interior plant species selection
Elevator cab finishes exclusively

explanation: Core Concepts ensure transparent, repeatable savings determination.

prompt: Sampling in M&V plans may be acceptable when:

options.text: Representative samples and statistical methods justify extrapolation to similar units
One light fixture represents an entire campus with no justification
No documentation of sample selection is provided
Samples contradict installed conditions deliberately

explanation: IPMVP allows sampling with proper design and uncertainty analysis.

prompt: Avoided cost in ESPC analysis represents:

options.text: The cost of energy the owner would have paid without the ECMs
The ESCO's office rent exclusively
Unrelated capital unrelated to utilities
Only water sewer charges with no energy component ever

explanation: Savings dollars multiply avoided energy units by applicable utility rates or tariffs.

distractorExplanations.b: Avoided cost is utility cost avoided.
distractorExplanations.c: Office rent is overhead, not avoided utility cost.
distractorExplanations.d: Energy components usually dominate ESPC savings.

prompt: Measurement uncertainty in M&V should be:

options.text: Estimated and documented so stakeholders understand confidence in reported savings
Ignored because exact precision is always achievable
Hidden from owners and utilities always
Larger than 100% without explanation

explanation: Uncertainty analysis supports defensible claims and contract remedies.

distractorExplanations.c: Transparency is required in good M&V.
distractorExplanations.d: Large uncertainty must be explained and mitigated.

prompt: Selecting a baseline period for M&V should consider:

options.text: Representative operation, complete data, and absence of major anomalies or concurrent ECMs
The week of a plant shutdown fire drill only if abnormal
Any partial month with missing bills preferred
A period after all retrofits are installed

explanation: Baseline must reflect pre-retrofit conditions for valid comparison.

distractorExplanations.d: Post-retrofit data defines reporting period, not baseline.

prompt: Operational ECMs in ESPCs differ from capital ECMs because operational measures:

options.text: Rely heavily on control changes, O&M practices, and persistence rather than new equipment alone
Never require training or monitoring
Always have zero cost to implement
Cannot be measured or verified

explanation: Controls optimization and RCx measures are common operational ECMs with low capital cost.

distractorExplanations.d: Many can be verified with trending and M&V.

prompt: Shared savings contract structures split:

options.text: Measured savings between owner and ESCO per agreed percentages after costs
Only landscaping maintenance fees
Profits from unrelated real estate ventures
HVAC filter sales exclusively

explanation: Shared savings align incentives when guarantee structures differ from standard ESPC loan amortization.

distractorExplanations.c: Real estate profits are separate businesses.
distractorExplanations.d: Filter sales are not ESPC core.

prompt: Peer review of an M&V plan helps:

options.text: Identify gaps in baseline, boundaries, and methods before implementation
Eliminate all measurement requirements automatically
Replace owner approval entirely
Guarantee double-counting of savings

explanation: Independent review improves defensibility for owners, ESCOs, and utilities.

distractorExplanations.b: Review strengthens plans; it does not remove M&V.
distractorExplanations.d: Peer review aims to prevent double counting.

prompt: A commissioning issues log tracks:

options.text: Deficiencies, responsible parties, resolution dates, and retest status
Only lunch orders for the Cx team
Unrelated stock market prices
Employee vacation schedules exclusively

explanation: Issues logs drive closure of items found during TAB, FPT, and documentation review.

distractorExplanations.b: Food orders are not Cx documentation.

prompt: Trend verification during retrocommissioning confirms:

options.text: Control sequences and setpoints match intended operation over time
Only that labels on valves are spelled correctly
Structural steel mill certificates exclusively
Carpet pile height in corridors

explanation: Trends reveal hunting, simultaneous heat/cool, and economizer lockout.

distractorExplanations.b: Spelling checks are minor admin tasks.

prompt: Test script development for commissioning should be based on:

options.text: Design intent, sequences of operation, and manufacturer requirements
Random equipment selection without OPR
Elimination of all safety interlock tests
Only visual inspection with no functional modes

explanation: Scripts define pass/fail criteria for each mode and interlock.

distractorExplanations.b: OPR and SOO guide test scope.

prompt: Owner acceptance in commissioning occurs when:

options.text: Systems meet OPR/design intent and outstanding issues are resolved or deferred with documentation
Contractor self-certifies with no owner review
All testing is skipped for schedule
Training and O&M deliverables are intentionally withheld

explanation: Acceptance follows verified performance and complete handoff documentation.

distractorExplanations.c: Testing cannot be skipped for acceptance.

prompt: IPMVP Option D relies primarily on:

options.text: Calibrated computer simulation models to predict baseline and reporting period consumption
A single handheld light meter reading only
Guesswork without calibration to utility data
Elimination of all operational schedules in the model

explanation: Option D suits complex sites when isolation metering is impractical but models must be calibrated.

distractorExplanations.c: Models must calibrate to utility or submeter data.

prompt: Savings persistence monitoring after RCx ensures:

options.text: Energy improvements continue rather than degrading due to overrides or poor O&M
All controls are permanently overridden to manual
Trending and meters are disabled after RCx
Operators receive no guidance on maintained setpoints

explanation: Persistence tracking catches backsliding that erodes RCx savings.

prompt: Commissioning training for O&M staff should cover:

options.text: Normal operation, alarms, seasonal changeover, and documentation locations
Only corporate HR policies unrelated to systems
How to disable all life safety devices
Deletion of BAS trend histories on day one

explanation: Training enables staff to operate systems as commissioned.

distractorExplanations.b: HR policies do not replace systems training.
distractorExplanations.c: Life safety must never be disabled.

prompt: ESPC termination clauses address:

options.text: Early contract end, performance shortfalls, and equipment ownership transfer
Only cafeteria vending machine restocking
Mandatory removal of all windows
Prohibition of any M&V during the term

explanation: Termination provisions protect both owner and ESCO if projects end prematurely.

distractorExplanations.c: Windows are unrelated to ESPC termination.

prompt: Interactive effects between ECMs occur when:

options.text: Combined savings differ from the sum of individual measure savings due to overlapping impacts
Measures are always perfectly additive with no analysis needed
Only one ECM can exist per building
M&V ignores all overlap by default without documentation

explanation: M&V plans must address interactions to avoid over-claiming savings.

prompt: Static factors in IPMVP include:

options.text: Parameters held constant or adjusted such as floor area, production rate, or operating hours
Only dynamic weather variables exclusively with no other factors
Stock market indices by requirement
Paint sheen levels in offices

explanation: Static factors change independently of ECM performance and need documented adjustment.

distractorExplanations.b: Weather is dynamic; static factors are separate category.

prompt: LEED fundamental commissioning overlaps with CEM-relevant practice by requiring:

options.text: Design review, commissioning plan, FPT, and O&M handoff for specified systems
No verification of HVAC controls ever
Exclusive focus on site parking counts
Elimination of all commissioning documentation

explanation: LEED EA credits include commissioning activities aligned with industry commissioning scope.

prompt: Monitoring-based commissioning (MBCx) uses:

options.text: Permanent or semi-permanent sensors and analytics to detect efficiency degradation
Only one-time walkthrough with no data
Elimination of all alarms in the BAS
Manual data entry once per decade only

explanation: MBCx automates fault detection to sustain savings between RCx events.

prompt: Fault detection in ongoing commissioning may identify:

options.text: Stuck dampers, failed economizers, and short cycling equipment from trend rules
Only aesthetic cracks in drywall
Unrelated personnel badge colors
Roof membrane brand preferences

explanation: Analytics on BAS and meter data reveal operational faults wasting energy.

distractorExplanations.b: Drywall cracks are not FDD targets.

prompt: Weather normalization using heating degree days (HDD) adjusts:

options.text: Heating energy comparisons for colder or milder reporting periods versus baseline
Refrigerant charge in DX units directly
Motor nameplate horsepower automatically
Filter MERV ratings for compliance

explanation: HDD-based models separate weather-driven consumption from ECM impacts.

distractorExplanations.c: Nameplate does not change with weather.

prompt: Commissioning cost-benefit analysis for owners should include:

options.text: Avoided energy, maintenance, comfort complaints, and equipment life impacts
Only the color of Cx report covers
Unrelated legal fees from other divisions always
Ignore any operational savings from corrected sequences

explanation: RCx and new Cx often pay back through energy and reduced call-backs.

distractorExplanations.b: Report aesthetics are negligible cost factor.
distractorExplanations.c: Unrelated legal fees should not be bundled without cause.

prompt: IPMVP Option A is best suited when:

options.text: Retrofit isolation uses short-term or periodic measurement of key parameters and agreed factors
Whole-building regression is the only acceptable approach always
No key parameter can be measured ever in any case
Baseline documentation is prohibited

explanation: Option A combines measured parameters with stipulated factors for short-term ECMs.

distractorExplanations.b: Option C emphasizes whole-building utility data.
distractorExplanations.c: Some parameters must be measured or stipulated with justification.
